description_long: This object is a large, rectangular stone block with a surface pattern resembling red clay bricks, each separated by thin mortar lines. The block's faces are uniformly covered in a brickwork texture, with subtle variations in color and surface wear, giving it a slightly weathered appearance. The bricks are arranged in a staggered, running bond pattern, typical of urban construction. The surface shows some minor discoloration and roughness, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. The block is solid and robust, likely very heavy, and would serve as a substantial barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. There are no visible openings, engravings, or interactive features on any side.
description: A large, solid rectangular block with a red brick-patterned surface and visible mortar lines, showing slight weathering. The block appears robust and is designed to serve as a substantial urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front face of the block showing a uniform red brick pattern with visible mortar lines and some surface wear.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same brick pattern and slight surface discoloration.
description_view_2: Another side view, consistent with the previous faces, showing the same brick arrangement and weathering.
description_view_3: Rear face of the block, also covered in the same red brick pattern, with slightly less visible surface wear.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative block
attribute_installation_method: set in mortar or concrete foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Slight surface discoloration and minor roughness, indicating some exposure to outdoor elements but no significant erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall or barrier
